page: Microsoft Azure M8-2ms is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MS server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8280M processor operating at 2.7 GHz. This configuration provides 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled, resulting in 2 threads per core. The processor includes a 38 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache, supporting instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512F, AES, and FMA.

The defining characteristic of the M8-2ms is its substantial memory capacity, offering 219.0 GB of RAM, which equates to 109.38 GB per physical core. This high memory-to-vCPU ratio is paired with 262 GB of local storage and premium storage capabilities. No GPUs are included in this configuration, and it does not bundle complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates a clear tradeoff between massive memory capacity and compute performance. The server exhibits low processing and memory bandwidth speeds, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance at 1031.38 and multi-core at 1354.57, placing it in the bottom tier of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is also limited, measuring 8.95 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 7.8 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Redis SET operations reach 1.31 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is recorded at 1.62 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the M8-2ms represents a specialized trade-off, providing high memory density at the expense of raw computational speed. It is designed for memory-intensive workloads that require large datasets to reside entirely in RAM but do not demand high CPU throughput. Appropriate workloads include memory-heavy database caching, small-scale in-memory databases, and enterprise applications with large memory footprints but low concurrent processing requirements.
description: Microsoft Azure M8-2ms is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8280M processor with 2 vCPUs, 1 physical core, and a 2.7 GHz clock speed. The server is characterized by an exceptionally high memory capacity of 219.0 GB and includes 262 GB of local storage with premium storage support. Benchmark results show a significant tradeoff, with CPU and memory bandwidth performance falling into the bottom tier of tested systems, including a stress-ng multi-core score of 1354.57 and large-block read bandwidth of 8.95 GB/sec. Consequently, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient specifically for specialized, memory-intensive workloads that require massive RAM capacity but have minimal computational demands, such as in-memory caching and database hosting.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M8-2ms is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 219 GB of RAM, 2 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 262 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads with low compute requirements.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M8-2ms is a memory-optimized VM with 2 vCPUs, 219 GB of RAM, and 262 GB of local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-intensive virtual machine offering massive RAM capacity and local storage for low-compute, memory-heavy enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Features 219.0 GB of RAM providing an exceptionally high memory-to-vCPU ratio.
Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8280M processor with 2 vCPUs and 1 physical core.
Includes 262 GB of local storage with premium storage capabilities.
Delivers 1.31 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks.
Best suited for memory-intensive caching and small-scale in-memory databases.
